title: Incursa.Quic Public API Surface Promotion Work Item
summary: Promote the connection-first Incursa.Quic consumer facade, refine its lifecycle semantics from Microsoft test evidence, and hide the helper-layer implementation seams.
planned_changes:
- Add the public connection, stream, options, error, and abort/direction types named in the slice.
- Correct `QuicStreamType` to the approved direction-only public model.
- Keep the packet, frame, TLS, handshake, runtime, recovery, congestion, stream-identity, and lifecycle helper surfaces internal.
- Reconcile the public API analyzer files with the approved smaller public boundary.
- Preserve the listener/TLS-auth work for the next slice and avoid broadening the public contract into middleware.
- Encode the close/dispose, error projection, read-side, and option-shape semantics reflected by the Microsoft reference tests and the existing runtime/stream-state seams.
out_of_scope: Adding listener/socket wiring in this pass.
Adding the full TLS-auth contract in this pass.
Adding new transport features beyond the approved facade.
verification_plan: Use API-diff and reflection-based surface checks, plus compile-time access tests, to prove the approved public types are visible and the listed seam types are not. Add focused consumer-behavior tests for `QuicStreamType` shape, close/dispose/error projection, read-side terminal behavior, and the connection/stream facade mapping to `QuicConnectionRuntime` and `QuicConnectionStreamState`. Keep the proof focused on surface shape rather than wire behavior; this slice does not require listener, connect/listen, TLS-auth, fuzzing, or benchmarks.
completion_notes: This item exists to promote the consumer facade only. The internal transport engine stays intact.

supplemental_sections.heading: Reference Behavioral Evidence
supplemental_sections.content:
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicListenerTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicConnectionTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\QuicStreamConnectedStreamConformanceTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicCipherSuitesPolicyTests.cs`
- `C:\src\dotnet\runtime\src\libraries\System.Net.Quic\tests\FunctionalTests\MsQuicTests.cs`

These reference tests are the behavioral evidence for the initial public surface shape and the lifecycle semantics that still need to be implemented and proven in the target repo.
